title: Imaging Mass Cytometry Compensation Slide Acquisition
author: Michelle Daniel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bf2jjqcn

steps.title: Attach the flat-bed scanner to a...
steps.instruction: Attach the flat-bed scanner to a computer and take an image of the compensation slide.

steps.title: Create a folder on the CyTOF...
steps.instruction: Create a folder on the CyTOF computer on the “E” drive.

steps.title: Save the image of the compensation...
steps.instruction: Save the image of the compensation slide in that folder with the same name as the compensation slide.

steps.title: Insert the compensation slide into the Hyperion
steps.instruction: Insert the compensation slide into the Hyperion.

steps.title: Under Data Acquisition create a new File (“Empty12AF”)
steps.instruction: Under Data Acquisition create a new File (“Empty12AF”).

steps.title: Save the mcd file in the newly created folder
steps.instruction: Save the mcd file in the newly created folder.

steps.title: Create a panorama across all the spots
steps.instruction: Create a panorama across all the spots. Potentially, the spots may be hard to see in the actual camera view. Requires a tuned and running IMC. For tuning see: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bf2gjqbw

steps.title: For each of the spots, create...
steps.instruction: For each of the spots, create an ROI with the size of 10x200 (10 lines with 200 pixels).

steps.title: Name each ROI with the metal...
steps.instruction: Name each ROI with the metal name of the conjugate (e.g. “Gd156”, “Ir191” etc).

steps.title: Apply a panel with the same...
steps.instruction: Apply a panel with the same (or more) channels and settings as measured or will be measured for the experiment to be compensated. When creating the panel, make sure to actually select the correct metal isotopes as this is important for the spill-over matrix generation.
